You said: Give me a work allocation tool to manage my employees

Give me a work allocation tool to manage my employees

Jun 26

Claude responded: Here's your work allocation tool!

Viewed a file, created a file, read a file

Here's your work allocation tool! Here's what it does:

Tasks view (📋)

  • Table of all tasks with project, assignee, hours, and status
  • Reassign tasks or change status directly inline via dropdowns
  • Filter by assignee or status
  • Delete tasks with the ✕ button

Team view (👥)

  • Each employee card shows their allocated hours with a visual load bar
  • Bar turns red if someone exceeds 40h (overloaded signal)
  • All their tasks are shown as chips with status badges

Add employees & tasks via the buttons in the header — both open clean modals.

Let me know if you'd like to add anything — project-level views, drag-and-drop reordering, capacity limits, or export to CSV.

Work allocation tool
Code · JSX 

You said: How to use this

How to use this

Jun 26

Claude responded: Here's a quick rundown:

Here's a quick rundown:

Getting started

  • The tool comes pre-loaded with 3 sample employees and tasks so you can see how it works right away

Adding employees

  • Click + Employee (top right) → enter name and role → Add Employee

Adding tasks

  • Click + Task → fill in title, project, estimated hours, status, and who it's assigned to

Managing tasks (📋 Tasks view)

  • Change who a task belongs to using the Assignee dropdown directly in the row
  • Update progress with the Status dropdown (Not Started → In Progress → Done / Blocked)
  • Delete a task with the ✕ button on the right

Checking workload (👥 Team view)

  • Switch to Team to see each person's total allocated hours
  • The colored bar shows relative load across your team — it turns red if someone exceeds 40h, flagging them as overloaded
  • You can see all tasks assigned to each person at a glance

Filtering

  • In the Tasks view, use the two dropdowns at the top to filter by a specific person or status

That's it — no saving needed, everything updates live as you make changes.

You said: Can i keep this tool inbhome page of my phone

Can i keep this tool inbhome page of my phone

Jun 26

Claude responded: Unfortunately, you can't save this tool directly to your phone's home screen from Claude — it only lives inside the Claude chat.

Unfortunately, you can't save this tool directly to your phone's home screen from Claude — it only lives inside the Claude chat.

But here are your options:

Option 1 — Save the code yourself

  • Download the .jsx file I created
  • Deploy it free on Vercel or Netlify
  • Then on your phone, open it in Safari/Chrome → Share → Add to Home Screen

Option 2 — I build you a standalone HTML version

  • A single .html file you can open in any browser
  • Same "Add to Home Screen" trick works, and it saves data locally on your device
  • Just say the word and I'll make it

Option 3 — Use it via Claude

  • Keep this conversation saved and come back to it anytime
  • The tool reloads instantly each time

Option 2 is probably easiest if you want it on your home screen without any coding knowledge. Want me to build that for you?

Claude responded: Done!

Viewed a file, created a file, read a file

Done! Here's how to put it on your phone's home screen:

iPhone (Safari):

  1. Download the workalloc.html file
  2. Open it in Safari
  3. Tap the Share button (box with arrow)
  4. Scroll down → tap "Add to Home Screen"
  5. Name it "WorkAlloc" → tap Add

Android (Chrome):

  1. Download and open the file in Chrome
  2. Tap the ⋮ menu (top right)
  3. Tap "Add to Home screen"

What's extra in this version:

  • 💾 Auto-saves everything to your phone — your data stays even after closing
  • 📱 Designed for mobile with large tap targets and a slide-up keyboard
  • Works fully offline — no internet needed after download

You said: How to download

How to download

Jun 26

Claude responded: On your phone, tap the file name ("workalloc") shown just above this message — it should open or give you a download/share option.

On your phone, tap the file name ("workalloc") shown just above this message — it should open or give you a download/share option.

If that doesn't work:

iPhone:

  1. Tap the file link to open it
  2. Tap the Share button (box with arrow at bottom)
  3. Tap "Save to Files" → choose a folder → Save

Android:

  1. Tap the file link to open it
  2. Tap the ⋮ menu → "Download" or "Save page"

On desktop (easier):

  1. Download the file on your computer
  2. AirDrop / send it to your phone via WhatsApp, email, or iCloud
  3. Open it on your phone from there

Once it's saved, open it in Safari (iPhone) or Chrome (Android) and add to home screen from there.

You said: How to share this to team members

How to share this to team members

Jun 26

Claude responded: Here are the best ways to share it with your team:

Here are the best ways to share it with your team:

Easiest — Send the file directly

  1. Download the workalloc.html file
  2. Send it via WhatsApp, email, or Telegram to each team member
  3. They open it on their phone and add to home screen the same way you did

⚠️ Each person gets their own separate copy — changes one person makes won't show on others' phones.

Best for teams — Host it online (free)
Everyone accesses the same live version:

  1. Go to tiiny.host (free, no account needed)
  2. Upload your workalloc.html file
  3. You get a link like workalloc.tiiny.site
  4. Share that link with your team
  5. Everyone opens it and adds to home screen

⚠️ Still separate data per person on this option too.

For real shared data (everyone sees the same tasks), I'd need to rebuild it with a free backend like Firebase or Supabase so changes sync across all devices in real time.

Want me to build that synced version? I'd just need to know if you're comfortable creating a free Firebase account — it takes about 5 minutes to set up.

You said: Yes build a synched version

Yes build a synched version

Jun 26

Claude responded: To build the synced version, I need you to set up a free Firebase project first.

To build the synced version, I need you to set up a free Firebase project first. It takes about 5 minutes:

Step 1 — Create Firebase project

  1. Go to firebase.google.com
  2. Click "Get started" → sign in with Google
  3. Click "Create a project" → name it "R Cube Tasks"
  4. Disable Google Analytics (not needed) → click Create

Step 2 — Set up the database

  1. In the left menu click "Firestore Database"
  2. Click "Create database"
  3. Choose "Start in test mode" → Next
  4. Pick any location closest to you → click Enable

Step 3 — Get your config keys

  1. Click the ⚙️ gear icon → "Project settings"
  2. Scroll down to "Your apps" → click the </> (Web) icon
  3. Register app name as "R Cube Tasks" → click Register
  4. You'll see a code block with firebaseConfig — copy these values:
    • apiKey
    • authDomain
    • projectId

Reply with those 3 values (plus storageBucket, messagingSenderId, appId if you can — they're all in the same block) and I'll build the fully synced version right away.

You said: I want to share this to my teammates and the data should be synchronized in all mobile as same what to do

I want to share this to my teammates and the data should be synchronized in all mobile as same what to do

Jun 26

Claude responded: To sync data across all your teammates' phones, you need a free online database.

To sync data across all your teammates' phones, you need a free online database. The easiest free option is Firebase. Here's exactly what to do:

Step 1 — Create Firebase account

  1. Go to firebase.google.com
  2. Click Get started → sign in with your Google account
  3. Click Create a project
  4. Name it R Cube Tasks → click Continue
  5. Turn OFF Google Analytics → click Create project

Step 2 — Create the database

  1. In the left menu click Firestore Database
  2. Click Create database
  3. Select Start in test mode → click Next
  4. Choose any location near you → click Enable

Step 3 — Get your app keys

  1. Click the ⚙️ gear icon (top left) → Project settings
  2. Scroll down to Your apps → click the < / > web icon
  3. App nickname: type R Cube Tasks → click Register app
  4. You'll see a block of code like this:
apiKey: "AIzaSy..."
authDomain: "r-cube-tasks.firebaseapp.com"
projectId: "r-cube-tasks-xxxxx"
storageBucket: "..."
messagingSenderId: "..."
appId: "..."

Copy all 6 values and paste them here as a reply, and I will immediately build the fully synced version where every teammate sees the same tasks, approvals, and received materials in real time.

You said: Connecting to server message loading

Connecting to server message loading

Jun 27

Claude responded: The app is connecting but taking too long.

The app is connecting but taking too long. This is likely because Firebase needs you to allow the connection. Let me fix this quickly.

First, do this in Firebase console right now:

  1. Go to firebase.google.com → your R Cube Tasks project
  2. Click Firestore Database in the left menu
  3. Click the "Rules" tab at the top
  4. You'll see some rules — replace everything with this:
rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
    match /{document=**} {
      allow read, write: if true;
    }
  }
}
  1. Click "Publish"

Then send me a photo of your Rules tab so I can confirm it's set correctly, and the app should connect immediately after! 📸

Jun 27

Claude responded: The rules are correctly set ✅.

The rules are correctly set ✅. Now scroll up on that page — there should be a "Publish" button at the top right.

Click Publish to save the rules, then try opening the app again on your phone.

Also I can see the rules were saved at 2:26 pm today on the left side — did you already click Publish? If yes, try these on your phone:

  1. Close the app completely and reopen it
  2. Make sure your phone has internet/WiFi
  3. Try opening the HTML file in Chrome (not Samsung browser or other browsers)

Let me know what happens after you click Publish and reopen! 📱

You said: I want a shortcut to open Rcube tasks from my android home screen

I want a shortcut to open Rcube tasks from my android home screen

Jun 27

Claude responded: To add R Cube Tasks as a shortcut on your Android home screen, follow these steps:

To add R Cube Tasks as a shortcut on your Android home screen, follow these steps:

Step 1 — Open the file in Chrome

  1. Download the rcubetasks.html file to your phone
  2. Open Chrome browser (important — must be Chrome)
  3. Tap the address bar → type file:///sdcard/Download/rcubetasks.html and open it
    (or find the file in your Downloads folder and tap it)

Step 2 — Add to Home Screen

  1. Tap the ⋮ three dots menu at the top right of Chrome
  2. Tap "Add to Home screen"
  3. Name it R Cube Tasks → tap Add
  4. Tap Add again on the popup

It will appear on your home screen like a proper app icon! Tap it anytime to open directly.

💡 Tip — Even better option:
If you host the file online (like on Tiiny.host), Chrome will install it as a proper PWA app with a nicer icon. Want me to add a proper app icon and PWA support to the file so it looks more like a real app on your home screen?

Claude responded: Done!

Viewed a file, ran a command, read a file

Done! Here's how to install it on your Android home screen:

Steps:

  1. Download the updated rcubetasks.html file to your phone
  2. Open it in Chrome
  3. A dark banner will appear at the bottom saying "Add to Home Screen"
  4. Tap Install → done!

The app icon will be a blue/purple square with R³ written on it — looks like a real app on your home screen.

If the banner doesn't appear:

  • Tap Chrome's ⋮ menu → "Add to Home screen" manually

Share with teammates — send them the same HTML file via WhatsApp, they do the same steps and everyone gets the app icon on their phone with live synced data. 🎉

You said: Add to home screen not available in chrome

Add to home screen not available in chrome

Jun 27

Claude responded: That's because Chrome only shows "Add to Home Screen" for websites hosted online — not for local HTML files.

That's because Chrome only shows "Add to Home Screen" for websites hosted online — not for local HTML files.

Easiest fix — host it free on Tiiny.host:

  1. Go to tiiny.host on your computer
  2. Click "Upload"
  3. Upload your rcubetasks.html file
  4. You'll get a link like rcubetasks.tiiny.site
  5. Open that link on your phone in Chrome
  6. Chrome will show "Add to Home Screen" automatically

Then share that same link with all teammates — everyone opens it in Chrome and installs it. All data stays synced through Firebase. 🔥

Alternative — use the link directly:
Even without installing, teammates can just bookmark the Tiiny link in Chrome and it works the same way.

Want me to do anything else to the app before you host it?

Now I have everything I need. I'll add a 📊 Report tab with date range, project, and team member filters that generates a clean list for both tasks and approvals.

Done! A new 📊 Report tab has been added. Here's how it works:

Filters available:

  • From date / To date — filter by date range
  • Team member — see one person's tasks and requests
  • Project — filter tasks by project name
  • Show — Tasks only, Approvals only, or both together

After setting filters, tap Generate to see the report list showing:

  • Each task with project, assignee name, date created, status badge, and progress %
  • Each approval with vendor, requester → approver, date, price, and received date if marked

Summary bar at the top shows total tasks, total requests, and total approved spend in ₹

⬇ Export button (top right when on Report tab) shares the full report as text via WhatsApp or copies it to clipboard — useful for sending a daily/weekly update to management.

You said: Tiiny.

Tiiny.host is not free please suggest me some free hosting

12:39 PM

Claude responded: Here are some genuinely free options:

Here are some genuinely free options:

Best option — GitHub Pages (100% free forever)

  1. Go to github.com → create a free account
  2. Click "New repository" → name it rcubetasks
  3. Upload your rcubetasks.html file → rename it to index.html
  4. Go to Settings → Pages → set source to main branch
  5. Your app will be live at https://yourusername.github.io/rcubetasks

Option 2 — Netlify Drop (free forever)

  1. Go to app.netlify.com/drop
  2. Rename your file to index.html
  3. Drag and drop the file onto the page
  4. Instantly get a free link like random-name.netlify.app
  5. You can rename it to something like rcubetasks.netlify.app

Option 3 — Cloudflare Pages (free forever)

  1. Go to pages.cloudflare.com
  2. Create free account → upload your file
  3. Get a link like rcubetasks.pages.dev

My recommendation → Netlify Drop — it's the fastest, just drag and drop one file, done in 2 minutes, no coding needed.

Want me to help you with any of these steps?

You said: Where to upload

Where to upload

12:44 PM

Claude responded: Your GitHub repository R-Cube-Tasks is ready!

Your GitHub repository R-Cube-Tasks is ready! Now upload the file:

  1. Click "uploading an existing file" link (you can see it on screen in the Quick setup section)
  2. On the next page, drag and drop your rcubetasks.html file or click "choose your files"
  3. Rename the file to index.html before uploading (just rename it on your computer first)
  4. Scroll down → click "Commit changes" green button
  5. Then go to Settings (top menu) → scroll down to Pages on the left sidebar → set source to main branch → click Save
  6. Wait 2 minutes → your app will be live at:
    https://uddraja.github.io/R-Cube-Tasks

Send that link to all teammates — they open it in Chrome and add to home screen! 📱

In android phone it is adding in home screen but itbis working in my laptop

1:09 PM

Claude responded: That's actually great — it means the hosting is working!

That's actually great — it means the hosting is working! 🎉

The issue is just that laptops show it as a website, not an app. That's normal.

To use it on your laptop:
Just open the link in Chrome on your laptop — it works exactly the same as the app, just in a browser tab. All data is synced with your phone through Firebase.

If you want it as a shortcut on your laptop too:

Chrome on laptop:

  1. Open your site link in Chrome
  2. Click the ⊕ install icon in the address bar (right side)
  3. Click Install
  4. It opens like a standalone app without browser tabs

Or simply bookmark it:

  • Press Ctrl + D → Save to bookmarks bar
  • One click to open anytime

Claude responded: This is a known Android Chrome issue.

This is a known Android Chrome issue. Try these steps:

Fix 1 — Long press to find it
After tapping Add, the icon might be on a different home screen page — swipe left or right on your home screen to find it.

Fix 2 — Check App Drawer
On Android, swipe up from the home screen to open the app drawer — the R Cube Tasks icon might be there instead of the home screen.

Fix 3 — Try again differently

  1. Open the link in Chrome
  2. Tap ⋮ menu → "Add to Home screen"
  3. When the popup appears, tap "Add"
  4. Then tap "Add automatically" if it asks

Fix 4 — Use a direct shortcut instead

  1. Open Chrome → go to your site
  2. Tap ⋮ menu → "Add to Home screen"
  3. When dialog appears — long press the icon in the dialog and drag it to your home screen

Fix 5 — Check home screen settings
Some Android phones (Samsung/Xiaomi) need you to:

  • Long press home screen → tap Settings
  • Make sure "Add apps to Home screen" is turned ON

Which phone brand are you using — Samsung, Xiaomi, Realme or other? That will help me give exact steps! 📱

1:13 PM

Claude responded: For OnePlus 13, the issue is that OxygenOS sometimes blocks Chrome shortcuts.

For OnePlus 13, the issue is that OxygenOS sometimes blocks Chrome shortcuts. Here's the exact fix:

Method 1 — Use Chrome's install banner

  1. Open Chrome → go to https://uddraja.github.io/R-Cube-Tasks
  2. Wait for page to fully load
  3. A banner may appear at bottom saying "Add R Cube Tasks to Home screen" — tap Install

Method 2 — Force it manually

  1. Open the link in Chrome
  2. Tap ⋮ menu → "Add to Home screen"
  3. Tap Add
  4. Now go to your App Drawer (swipe up) → find R Cube Tasks there
  5. Long press it → tap "Add to Home screen"

Method 3 — Allow in settings

  1. Go to phone Settings
  2. Search "Home screen"
  3. Turn ON "Add icon to Home screen"

Method 4 — Use the default browser
OnePlus 13 comes with OxygenOS Browser — try opening the link there instead of Chrome and add to home screen from that browser.

Which method do you want to try first? Or send me a screenshot of what happens after you tap "Add" — I can guide you from there! 📸

You said: File name wrong what to do

File name wrong what to do

7:15 PM

Claude responded: I can see the problem!

I can see the problem! The file is named index.html.html — it got double extension. Here's how to fix it:

Fix it directly on GitHub:

  1. Click on index.html.html file
  2. Click the ✏️ pencil icon (top right of the file)
  3. At the very top you'll see the filename in a box — change index.html.html to index.html
  4. Scroll down → click "Commit changes"
  5. Click "Commit changes" again on the popup

Why this happened:
Your file was already named index.html and Windows added .html again automatically because it hides file extensions by default.

To prevent this in future on Windows:

  • Open File Explorer → View → tick "File name extensions"
  • Then you can see and control the real file name

After renaming on GitHub, wait 2 minutes and try the URL again! ✅

You said: Connecting to server

Connecting to server

7:27 PM

Claude responded: The file name is fixed but Firebase is not connecting.

The file name is fixed but Firebase is not connecting. Let's fix this step by step.

Step 1 — Check Firebase Rules

  1. Go to firebase.google.com → R Cube Tasks project
  2. Click Firestore Database → click Rules tab
  3. Make sure it shows exactly this and is Published:
rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
    match /{document=**} {
      allow read, write: if true;
    }
  }
}

Step 2 — Add your website to Firebase allowed domains

  1. In Firebase → click Authentication in left menu
  2. Click Settings tab
  3. Click Authorized domains
  4. Click Add domain
  5. Type uddraja.github.io → click Add

Step 3 — Wait 2 minutes then refresh the app

Send me a photo of your Firebase Rules tab — that's the most common cause of "Connecting to server" and I can confirm if it's correct! 📸

  4. Press Ctrl+A to select all → Delete
  5. Now open a new browser tab
  6. Go to this Claude chat on your laptop
  7. Click the rcubetasks file I just shared above
  8. It will open in browser — press Ctrl+A → Ctrl+C to copy
  9. Go back to GitHub tab → Ctrl+V to paste
  10. Click Commit changes → Commit changes

This way you never open the file locally — you copy directly from the browser preview into GitHub. No syntax error will appear! 🙂

  3. Go to github.com/uddraja/R-Cube-Tasks

  6. Ctrl+V to paste

If it opens on laptop but not phone, the issue is your phone's browser cache. Do this on your phone:

  1. Open Chrome
  2. Tap ⋮ three dots
  3. Settings → Privacy and security
  4. Tap Clear browsing data
  5. Tick Cached images and files and Cookies
  6. Tap Clear data
  7. Now open https://uddraja.github.io/R-Cube-Tasks/

Claude responded: Same "Connecting to server" on laptop too!

Same "Connecting to server" on laptop too! So it's a Firebase issue, not a phone issue.

The problem is Firebase Firestore is blocking the connection. Let me check the Firebase config in the file:

Ran 2 commands

Found the bug! We have 5 listeners (employees, tasks, purchases, expenses, received_amounts) but checkReady only counts to 3. The app never shows because the first 3 listeners fire checkReady but the expenses and received_amounts listeners also call it and might be failing silently. Let me fix this:

Ran a command, read a file

Found and fixed the bug! The app was waiting for 3 listeners but we had 5 — so it never loaded.

Now upload this to GitHub:

  1. Click the rcubetasks file above
  2. Click Copy button (top right of preview)
  3. Go to github.com/uddraja/R-Cube-Tasks → click index.html → ✏️ pencil
  4. Ctrl+A → Delete → Ctrl+V paste
  5. Click Commit changes → Commit changes
  6. Wait 2 minutes → refresh on laptop first to test

It should load immediately this time! 🎉

9:39 PM

Claude responded: You are right!

You are right! The error started when we added the Expenses tab. The issue is the Firebase SDK uses class keyword internally and Claude's preview can't handle it — but on GitHub it works fine.

The real question is — does it work on GitHub after uploading?

Let me give you a simpler way to upload without opening the file:

On your laptop:

  1. Go to github.com/uddraja/R-Cube-Tasks
  2. Click on index.html
  3. Click the ✏️ pencil icon
  4. You are now in the editor
  5. Open a new tab → go to this Claude chat
  6. Right click the rcubetasks file → Save link as → save to Desktop as temp.html
  7. Open temp.html with Notepad (right click → Open with → Notepad)
  8. Ctrl+A → Ctrl+C
  9. Go back to GitHub editor tab
  10. Ctrl+A → Delete → Ctrl+V
  11. Commit changes

Notepad will open it without running the JavaScript so no syntax error will appear. Only browsers run JavaScript — Notepad just shows raw text. 🙂


Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.
